sentences.text: Voice Therapy Number: 0646 Applicable CPT / HCPCS / ICD-10 Codes Scope of Policy This Clinical Policy Bulletin addresses voice therapy.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: I. Medical Necessity Aetna considers voice therapy medically necessary to restore the ability of the member to produce speech sounds from the larynx for any of the following indications: A. Essential voice tremor; or B. Following surgery or traumatic injury to the vocal cords; or C. Following treatment for laryngeal (glottic) carcinoma; or D. Muscle tension dysphonia (functional dysphonia); or E. Paradoxical vocal cord motion; or F. Post-laryngectomy (esophageal speech production); or G. Spastic (spasmodic) dysphonia; or H. Symptomatic benign vocal fold lesions (cysts, nodules and polyps); or I. Vocal cord paralysis.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Aetna considers voice therapy not medically necessary for any of the following indications: A. Improvement of voice quality; or B. Occupational or recreational purposes (e.g., public speaking, singing, etc.);
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: or C. Self-limited conditions, such as acute laryngitis.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Maintenance treatment, where the member's symptoms are not improving, is considered not medically necessary.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: If no clinical benefit is appreciated after 4 weeks of voice therapy, then the treatment plan should be re-evaluated.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Further voice therapy is not considered medically necessary if the member does not demonstrate meaningful improvement in symptoms.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Note: Megaphones or amplifiers (e.g., ChatterVox, Mega Mite Megaphone) may be of use in the absence of illness or injury and therefore do not meet Aetna's definition of covered durable medical equipment.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Note: An electronic artificial larynx (artificial voice box) that is used by laryngectomized individuals and persons with a permanently inoperative larynx is covered as a prosthetic.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: See "Note" regarding electronic speech aids accompanying CPB 0437 - Speech Generating Devices.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: See also CPB 0560 - Voice Prosthesis for Voice Rehabilitation Following Total Laryngectomy.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Note: Voice therapy for male-to-female transgender individuals to feminize the voice or for female-to- male transgender individuals to masculinize the voice is considered not medically necessary and cosmetic.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: See also CPB 0615 - Gender Affirming Surgery. II.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven Aetna considers voice therapy experimental, investigational, or unproven for the following because their effectiveness has not been established: Pre-operative voice therapy for improving outcomes of surgery for benign vocal fold lesions; Vocal fold motion impairment following chemotherapy administration. III.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Policy Limitations and Exclusions Note: Voice therapy is subject to any benefit limitations and exclusions applicable to speech therapy. IV.
sentences.label: CLAIM

R47.89 Other speech disturbances Z51.89 Encounter for other specified aftercare [occupational therapy] Vocal cord paralysis is a voice disorder that occurs when one or both of the vocal cords (or vocal folds) do not open or close properly (NIDCD, 1999).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Vocal cord paralysis is a common disorder, and symptoms can range from mild to life-threatening.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Someone who has vocal cord paralysis often has difficulty swallowing and coughing because food or liquids slip into the trachea and lungs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This happens because the paralyzed cord or cords remain open, leaving the airway passage and the lungs unprotected.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Vocal cord paralysis may be caused by head trauma, a neurological insult such as a stroke, a neck injury, lung or thyroid cancer, a tumor pressing on a nerve, or a viral infection (NIDCD, 1999).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In older people, vocal cord paralysis is a common problem affecting voice production.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: People with certain neurological conditions, such as multiple sclerosis or Parkinson's disease, or people who have had a stroke may experience vocal cord paralysis.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In many cases, however, the cause is unknown.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE
